canonical_question: How do I apply the two-pointer technique?
aliases: two pointer pattern for arrays and linked lists
constraints: Opposite-direction pointers require sorted input or order-independent problem
Fast/slow pointer cycle detection assumes singly linked list with single cycle entry
Never mutate collection from both pointer positions without a dedicated write pointer
Two pointers cannot replace hash map for pair-sum on unsorted arrays without sorting first
Always guard against off-by-one: ensure both pointers stay within bounds every iteration

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need subarrays with dynamic size constraint (e.g., longest substring with at most K distinct characters)
Need to search for a single element in a sorted collection
Need O(1) lookup for pair-sum in an unsorted array
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use a hash map / hash set approach

often_confused_with.label: Sliding Window Technique (same-direction pointers with a window, not a pair)

inputs_needed.question: Which two-pointer variant does your problem require?
What data structure are you working with?
